mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 7.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth expectations, boosting consumer spending power and driving immediate positive equity returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 7.0, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real GDP and housing starts signals broad-based economic expansion. The joint positive movement in GDPC1 and HOUST confirms aggregate demand strength, which lifts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term (GDPC1 growth × HOUST growth) shows a negative or statistically insignificant coefficient in a multivariate regression of 20-day SPX returns, or forward returns are flat when both series rise simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain resilience. During late-cycle expansions, this supply-side strength prevents inflationary bottlenecks, allowing equities to rally on earnings growth rather than rate fears, producing positive 60-day forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns turn negative when INDPRO rises above its 12-month moving average during late-cycle phases, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in post-2008 data.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the CPIAUCSL exceeding 0.2 percent triggers inflation hedging flows into equities. This threshold breach signals that nominal earnings growth will outpace discount rate increases, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns decline when CPIAUCSL breaches the 0.2 percent monthly threshold, or the signal produces a negative Rank IC during periods where the Federal Reserve explicitly targets inflation containment.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales and financial assets (RSAFS) reflect increasing household liquidity and consumption capacity. This continuous demand expansion directly supports corporate top-line growth, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 prices over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The rolling 12-month correlation between RSAFS changes and 5-day SPX returns falls below 0.1, or forward returns show no statistical difference from zero across all market conditions.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of rising nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment confirms structural labor market healing. This dual improvement reduces uncertainty premiums and supports sustained earnings revisions, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative or flat 60-day forward returns when PAYEMS rises and UNRATE falls simultaneously, or the interaction effect disappears after controlling for FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, a divergence where industrial production (INDPRO) outpaces GDP growth (GDPC1) signals supply-side efficiency gains that protect corporate margins. This specific regime dynamic drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns turn negative when INDPRO growth exceeds GDPC1 growth during Fed tightening periods, or the margin-protection mechanism fails to predict returns when rate hikes exceed 50 basis points per quarter.
